body:
I don't know about `jeffreytse/zsh-vi-mode`, but if it's `ohmyzsh`'s [`zsh-vi-mode`](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/a84a0332a822a78ddf3f66d0e1ed3990d4badd12/plugins/vi-mode/vi-mode.plugin.zsh#L28C1-L44C2), this seems achievable by checking the `VI_KEYMAP` variable:

> ```zsh
> # ohmyzsh/plugins/vi-mode/vi-mode.plugin.zsh 28~44
> function _vi-mode-set-cursor-shape-for-keymap() {
>   [[ "$VI_MODE_SET_CURSOR" = true ]] || return
>
>   # https://vt100.net/docs/vt510-rm/DECSCUSR
>   local _shape=0
>   case "${1:-${VI_KEYMAP:-main}}" in
>     main)    _shape=$VI_MODE_CURSOR_INSERT ;; # vi insert: line
>     viins)   _shape=$VI_MODE_CURSOR_INSERT ;; # vi insert: line
>     isearch) _shape=$VI_MODE_CURSOR_INSERT ;; # inc search: line
>     command) _shape=$VI_MODE_CURSOR_INSERT ;; # read a command name
>     vicmd)   _shape=$VI_MODE_CURSOR_NORMAL ;; # vi cmd: block
>     visual)  _shape=$VI_MODE_CURSOR_VISUAL ;; # vi visual mode: block
>     viopp)   _shape=$VI_MODE_CURSOR_OPPEND ;; # vi operation pending: blinking block
>     *)       _shape=0 ;;
>   esac
>   printf $'\e[%d q' "${_shape}"
> }
> ```

The mapping would look like this:

- `ShellEditMode::Insert`: `main`, `viins`, `isearch`, `command`
- `ShellEditMode::Normal`: `vicmd`
- `ShellEditMode::Visual`: `visual`
- `ShellEditMode::Replace`: *`viopp` maybe?*
- `ShellEditMode::ReplaceOne`: *`viopp` maybe?*

I could be wrong though, as I couldn't get this running on my pc
